summary: Install-time RCE dropper and host fingerprint exfiltration to bare-IP HTTP endpoint
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on `npm install` and performs two attacker-controlled actions. First, it collects host identifiers (process.platform, os.arch, os.hostname, os.userInfo().username, node version, process.cwd) and base64-encodes them into the query string of an HTTP GET to a bare IP at http://213.218.160.189:8080/s?q=<base64>. Second, it writes the response body to os.tmpdir()/.npm-cache/v-<timestamp>.js and spawns it with `spawn(process.execPath, [file], { detached: true, stdio: 'ignore', env: process.env })`, then deletes the file ~60s later to hide traces. The destination URL is obfuscated as a charCode array and decoded at runtime. There is no TLS, no integrity check, no signature verification, and the hidden cache directory plus self-cleanup are deliberate concealment. The package name `@izumiswap/sdk` impersonates the legitimate iZiSwap SDK published under izumiFinance and copies the real project's repository and homepage URLs as cover.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
